premise: Purple-team output: for the same triaged findings, ship detection-as-code that would catch the exploitation red-team is proving. Runs in parallel with red_team off the triage fan-out.

sigma.title: Webhook Signature Verification Failures Spiking From Single Source
sigma.detection: selection: msg='stripe.webhook.sig_verify' result='fail'; threshold: count() by src_ip > 20 in 1m; plus selection2: result='accept' after >5 prior fails from same src_ip
sigma.rationale: A timing/forgery attack generates many near-miss signature failures, then a sudden accept. Catches T-002 exploitation even before the secret is rotated.
falsepositives: Stripe retry storms after an outage (tune threshold)

sigma.title: SQL Error / UNION Tokens In Webhook Event Id
sigma.rationale: Flags injection payloads arriving in the event-id header (the T-001 source) at the edge, independent of whether the query is fixed yet.
falsepositives: legitimate event ids never contain SQL meta-characters — very low FP

yara.anchor: hardcoded signing-secret string prefix 'whsec_5f3b9c2a' (stable, unique artifact per YARA selection strategy)
yara.note: Scans build artifacts / images for the leaked secret so it is flagged anywhere it propagates until rotation + history purge complete. Defense-in-depth, not a fix.

coverage_map.T1190: covered (Sigma: SQL tokens in event id)
coverage_map.T1499.004: NOT covered — the parser-crash DoS (T-004) has no behavioral rule yet; logged as residual gap for the posture report

coverage_note: 3 of the 4 threat-intel techniques now have detection; residual gap on the availability DoS. Coverage is a COMPENSATING CONTROL for the gate — it does not waive a confirmed-exploitable blocker.
source: building-detection-rules-with-sigma (Purple Team Output -> Sigma; ATT&CK tagging) + performing-yara-rule-development-for-detection (artifact-anchored rule)
